patch.value:
Verification plan:
- Create a temporary directory outside the repository.
- Initialize a fresh pm project and create at least one closed item.
- Install pm-changelog from this checkout through pm install.
- Run pm changelog generate from the extension.
- Run the packed/linked standalone CLI against JSON input.
- Confirm generated output contains the temporary closed item.
Verify pm-changelog in a clean temporary project
Install the local package into an isolated temporary pm project and prove the extension command and standalone CLI generate changelog output without relying on repository-local state.

- Temporary project starts empty and is initialized independently.
- pm install from the local checkout succeeds.
- pm changelog generate writes a changelog containing the closed temporary item.
- Standalone pm-changelog CLI can consume JSON input and emit markdown.
- Temporary verification leaves the source repository clean except intentional release files.

value.command: tempdir pm init && pm install local checkout && pm changelog generate

patch.value: Verified the local checkout installs into a clean temporary pm project and both extension and standalone CLI generate changelog output for a closed temp item.
A fresh temporary project can install pm-changelog and generate changelog markdown through both supported entry points.

Temp project /tmp/pm-changelog-release-fEU4iG installed the package, package doctor had no warnings, pm changelog generate created CHANGELOG.md with one item, and standalone CLI stdout contained the same smoke item.

value.author: codex
value.text: Temp verification output: extension action=created changed=true item_count=1 bytes=99; CLI stdout bytes=97.

patch.value: Clean temporary project install and generation smoke test passed for extension and standalone CLI.
